title: The Great Barrier Reef
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The Great Barrier Reef, located off the coast of Australia, is the largest coral reef system in the world and can even be (0) seen from space. It (1) stretches for over 2,300 kilometres and is home to an incredible variety of marine life. Scientists estimate that the reef (2) supports approximately 1,500 species of fish and 400 types of coral. (3) Despite its impressive size, the reef faces serious environmental challenges. Rising sea temperatures have (4) led to widespread coral bleaching, a phenomenon that occurs when corals expel the algae living in their tissues. Conservation efforts are now (5) under way to protect this natural wonder. Marine biologists are working to (6) raise awareness about the importance of reducing carbon emissions. (7) Unless urgent action is taken, experts warn that up to 90% of the reef could be lost by 2050. The Australian government has (8) committed billions of dollars to preservation projects, hoping to save this unique ecosystem for future generations.

example.explanation: 'Can be seen' es la forma pasiva correcta para indicar visibilidad.

questions.explanation: 'Stretches' es el verbo adecuado para indicar la extensión física de algo largo.
'Supports' significa sostener o mantener vida, ideal para ecosistemas.
'Despite' va seguido de sustantivo y expresa contraste con lo anterior.
La expresión fija es 'led to', que significa 'conducir a' o 'provocar'.
'Under way' es la expresión fija que significa 'en marcha' o 'en curso'.
'Raise awareness' es la colocación correcta para 'concienciar' o 'crear conciencia'.
'Unless' significa 'a menos que' e introduce una condición negativa necesaria.
'Committed' se usa con dinero o recursos destinados a un propósito específico.
